key_points: The Mughal Empire became weak in the 18th century
Regional powers like Marathas, Sikhs, Nizams, and Rajputs gained strength
British and French trading companies set up posts and influenced local politics
Important battles like Plassey and Buxar changed political control
Political changes affected taxation, trade, and everyday life
examples: The British East India Company took control of Bengal after the Battle of Plassey
Marathas expanded across central and western India
Sikhs became powerful in Punjab during Mughal decline
Nizam of Hyderabad sometimes allied with Europeans to protect his territory
formulas: Mughal Decline → Rise of Regional Powers + European Influence
exam_tips: Remember the key regional powers and their locations
Know major battles and their outcomes
Understand the role of British and French companies
Focus on how political changes affected daily life
Use simple examples to support answers in exams
